content.full_text:
Farn Polruca.r, Pnacrrcrs CouvrrssroN
P.O. Box 807 . 428 J Street . Sacramento, CA 95812-0807
(916) 322-5660. Fax (916) 322-0886
July 23,2001
Diane Guyon
El Dorado County Elections Department
P. O. Box 678001
Placerville, CA 95667-8001
Re: Your Request for Informal Assistance
OurFileNo. I-01-174
DearMs. Guyon:
This letter is in response to your request for advice regarding the provisions of the
Political Reform Act (the "Act").r Since you are not requesting advice on the duties of a specific
public official, we are treating your request as one for informal assistance pursuant to Regulation
t8329(c);
QUESTION
The County of El Dorado is holding a ballot measure election on August 7,2001. The
election is for the purpose of issuing bonds to maintain, acquire, construct, and equip a new
recreational community center for the Cameron Park Community Services District and the ballot
desiguation is Measure C. Since the first and second pre-election statements include the
reporting period covered by the semi-annual statement due July 31,2001, may committees
formed to support or oppose this measure combine their semi-annual statement with their second
pre-election statement?
CONCLUSION
Yes. Any committee formed prior to June 30, 2001, for the purpose of supporting or
opposing your August7,200l, measure, may combine the semi-annual statement with the
second pre-election statement. This combined statement must be filed no later than Iuly 26,
2001, the deadline for the second pre-election statement.
rGovernment Code sections 81000-91014. Commission regulations appear attttle 2, sections 18109-
18995, of the California Code of Regulations.
2lnformal assistance does not provide the requestor with the immumty provided by an opinion or formal
written advice. (Government Code section 83114; 2 Cal. Code of Regs. section 18329(c)(3).)
@


Advice Letter No. I-01-174
Page Two
ANALYSIS
Committees formed to support or oppose a ballot measure are required to file trvo
campaign statements disclosing conhibutions received and expenditures made in connection with
that election. These pre-election campaign statements are generally filed 40 days before the
election and again 12 days before the election. (Section 84200.8.) Additionally, ballot measure
committees must file quarterly statements and semi-annual statements. Semi-annual statements
are filed January 3l and July 31, respectively. (Section 34200.)
The County of El Dorado is holding an election on August 7,2001. The first pre-election
statement filed in connection with this election is due June 28, 2001, and covers the reporting
period of January 1 through June 23. The second pre-election statement is due July 26,2001,
and covers the reporting period of June 24 through July 21,2001. Anyballot measure committee
formed prior to June 30, 2001, would also be required to file a semi-annual statement by July 3l
covering the period June24 through June 30.
There is a mechanism in the Act which permits the combining of statements when an
overlapping reporting period occurs or the filing of the semi-annual statement would be an
additional, unnecessary filing to the two pre-election statements. (Section 84205.) It appears
that combining the second pre-election staternent with the semi-annual statement would be
appropriate for the County of El Dorado's August7,2001, election.
Accordingly, any committee which came into existence prior to June 30, 2001, formed to
support or oppose the August 7,2001, ballot measure may combine its semi-annual statement
with its second pre-election statement. The date for filing this combined statement is July 26,
2001, the date the second pre-election statement is due. A campaign statement filing schedule
that outlines the due dates for filing statements is enclosed for your convenience.
If I may be of any further assistance, please do not hesitate to contact me at (916) 323-
693s
Enclosure
Sincerely,
Luisa Menchaca
General Counsel
By: Dixie Howard
Manager, Filing Officer Programs
Technical Assistance Division
